error.signature: File parsing fails or produces unexpected characters at the beginning despite looking correct in editor

verdict.summary: UTF-8 BOM (Byte Order Mark, 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF) is prepended by Windows editors (Notepad, Excel export). Most editors hide it, but parsers choke: JSON parsers reject it, CSV first column gets corrupted, shebang lines break.

dead_ends.action: Open file as UTF-8 and assume clean content
dead_ends.why_fails: UTF-8 with BOM inserts 3 invisible bytes at the start. json.loads fails. CSV first header gets \ufeff prefix. Dict lookups on first column fail.

dead_ends.action: Manually strip first 3 bytes from file
dead_ends.why_fails: Only works if BOM is present. Stripping 3 bytes from non-BOM file corrupts the first character.

workarounds.action: Open with encoding='utf-8-sig' in Python (auto-strips BOM if present)
workarounds.how: open('file.csv', encoding='utf-8-sig')  # strips BOM if present, works normally if absent

workarounds.action: Use dos2unix or sed to strip BOM from files in bulk
Configure editors to save as 'UTF-8 without BOM' by default
